pain_point_label: Asset Transfers Between Campuses Lack Formal Process and Tracking

matches.match_rationale: The core pain is the absence of a formal, documented workflow for inter-campus asset transfers — assets move physically without any system update. A task automation agent can orchestrate the end-to-end transfer workflow: triggering a transfer request, routing it for approval, updating the asset register at source and destination, sending confirmation, and generating an audit-trail entry — all without manual data entry. This directly addresses the ad-hoc, undocumented nature of transfers that creates register discrepancies and compliance risk.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-driven transfer workflow that intercepts a transfer request (email, form, or chat), extracts asset ID and destination campus, checks the asset register, routes for approval, and updates both campus registers — producing a timestamped audit record automatically.

matches.match_rationale: Asset transfer records across a multi-campus operator often exist in disparate, inconsistent formats — spreadsheets per campus, email chains, handwritten notes, or partial ERP exports. AI-assisted data transformation can normalize these fragmented records into a unified schema, map legacy asset IDs to a canonical register, and clean duplicate entries that arise when both campuses believe they own the same asset. This directly addresses the discrepancy and duplicate-record manifestation of the pain point.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A one-time (then recurring) pipeline that ingests each campus's asset register, normalises fields, detects cross-campus duplicates by asset ID or description, and outputs a reconciled master register with flagged transfer discrepancies for human review.

matches.match_rationale: After a unified asset register is in place, anomaly detection can continuously monitor for patterns that indicate undocumented transfers or phantom assets — for example, an asset suddenly appearing in stocktake data at Campus B while still listed at Campus A, or an IT asset whose last-seen location no longer matches its registered location. This turns the reactive stocktake-discovery problem into a proactive alert before audits or campus closures surface discrepancies.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A monitoring layer over the asset register that flags statistically abnormal location discrepancies or usage patterns (e.g. an asset scanned at a campus it is not registered to) and notifies facilities management for investigation and documentation.

matches.match_rationale: Auditors and management require formal documentation of each asset transfer — a transfer notice, a chain-of-custody record, and a summary for campus closure reports. Generating these documents from structured transfer data (who, what, when, from/to) removes the manual writing burden that discourages staff from documenting transfers in the first place. Lowering documentation friction increases compliance with the formal process.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Auto-generate transfer dockets, chain-of-custody memos, and campus closure asset-movement summaries from structured transfer records, ready for sign-off and audit filing.
